name: Arched lintel
description: An arched lintel spans an opening — door, window, or passage — by curving brick or stone voussoirs upward from the two jambs to meet at a central crown, transferring vertical load outward into compression along the arch and down into the supporting piers. Unlike a flat timber lintel that resists load in bending and is limited to short spans, the arched form scales gracefully to wider openings and reads as the structural signature of Romanesque (round arch), Gothic (pointed arch), and Mediterranean masonry traditions. In voxel form the smooth curve is approximated by a stepped pattern of stairs and full blocks, often with an optional keystone block at the crown that closes the arch and visually anchors the composition above the void it spans.
